name: Stuffed Cabbage with Rice & Pine Nuts Avgolemono
recipeInstructions:
1. Plunge the cabbage heads into boiling salted water and cook for about 8 minutes. Drain thoroughly and set aside while preparing the filling.
2. In a heavy skillet, heat the clarified butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and cook until soft and transparent.
3. Add the water and bring to a boil. Then add the rice and stir. Lower the heat and simmer gently for approximately 15 minutes, or until the rice has absorbed the liquid.
4. Remove from heat and stir in the raisins or currants, pine nuts, parsley, dill, salt, and pepper. Let it cool slightly.
5. Separate two of the eggs; mix the egg whites into the filling. Reserve the yolks for the avgolemono sauce.
6. Stuff each cabbage leaf with a heaping tablespoon of the filling. Roll up snugly and place seam side down in a casserole dish, dotting with butter.
7. Add water to cover the cabbage rolls, then cover the casserole with an inverted plate. Simmer for approximately 1 hour until tender. Transfer to a warm serving dish and keep warm.
8. Strain the remaining liquid into a bowl.
9. Beat the reserved egg yolks in a separate bowl, gradually adding the lemon juice. Continue beating and add the cooking liquid by droplets, stirring constantly, until all is incorporated.
10. Cook over hot water, not boiling, stirring constantly until the sauce thickens enough to coat a spoon. Pour over the cabbage rolls and serve hot.
